title: Claude's Fable 5 just solved an 87-year-old math problem, and it matters for bitcoin
content: Claude Fabel 5 disproved the Jacobian conjecture over the weekend, landing days after the capabilities of China's Kimi AI became the biggest force moving bitcoin markets.
summary: Claude Fabel 5 has reportedly disproved the 87-year-old Jacobian conjecture, a significant achievement in mathematics. This development follows closely after China's Kimi AI capabilities were identified as a major force influencing Bitcoin markets.

title: AI compute stocks bounce as Hut 8, IREN land billions in new contracts
content: The deals sparked a rebound in AI compute stocks after investors questioned demand for new data center capacity.
summary: Hut 8's stock surged significantly following a $9.8 billion long-term lease for an AI data center. This substantial deal has reinvigorated investor confidence in the AI compute sector, which had previously faced skepticism regarding the demand for new data center capacity.

title: Hut 8, IREN deals lift AI-focused Bitcoin mining stocks
content: Bitcoin miners rallied as investors cheered multi-billion-dollar AI infrastructure contracts, underscoring the sector’s accelerating shift toward data centers and cloud computing.
summary: Bitcoin mining stocks, including Hut 8 and IREN, rallied following the announcement of multi-billion-dollar AI infrastructure contracts. This development highlights an accelerating industry shift among Bitcoin miners towards data centers and cloud computing to support AI demands.

title: Galaxy sets up $5 million fund to help shield Bitcoin against quantum computing threats
content: The crypto financial services firm is funding developers to strengthen the blockchain's security before quantum computers become powerful enough to steal billions in crypto.
summary: Galaxy Digital, a crypto financial services firm, has established a $5 million fund to enhance Bitcoin's security. This initiative aims to prepare the blockchain against future threats posed by powerful quantum computers, which could potentially compromise cryptographic security and lead to the theft of billions in crypto. The fund will support developers working on these security improvements.

title: Russia’s parliament passes crypto market law with $3,800 annual cap for retail investors
content: While citizens still cannot use Bitcoin to buy groceries, the Kremlin is officially letting companies use digital tokens to bypass sanctions and other global trade hurdles.
summary: Russia's parliament has passed a law allowing companies to use digital tokens to bypass sanctions, with a $3,800 annual cap for retail investors, marking a significant development in the country's crypto market. The law does not permit citizens to use Bitcoin for everyday purchases. This move is seen as a way for Russia to navigate global trade hurdles.

title: Augustus raises $180 million to build a clearing bank for the AI and stablecoin era
content: The firm, freshly valued at $1 billion, aims to replace legacy correspondent banking with always-on infrastructure that connects traditional payment systems and stablecoins.
summary: Augustus has raised $180 million to create a clearing bank for the AI and stablecoin era, aiming to replace traditional correspondent banking with always-on infrastructure. The firm is now valued at $1 billion. This new infrastructure will connect traditional payment systems and stablecoins.

title: Bitcoin rally has broad-based support as institutions, whales, options traders pile in
content: Your day-ahead look for July 21, 2026
summary: Bitcoin is experiencing a broad-based rally, fueled by significant interest from institutional investors, large holders ('whales'), and options traders. This indicates strong and diverse market support for the cryptocurrency's upward trend.

title: Clarity odds jump to 43% on Polymarket after unverified reports Trump agreed to ethics deal
content: Bettors moved the crypto market structure bill to a 43% chance of passing this year, up from a record low last week, though no bill text has appeared.
summary: Bettors on Polymarket have increased the perceived likelihood of a crypto market structure bill passing this year to 43%, following unverified reports that former President Trump agreed to an ethics deal. This jump comes after a record low last week, though official bill text has not yet appeared.

title: Crypto markets rally on Clarity progress report, Asian chip-stock rebound
content: Speculation that President Donald Trump agreed to a crucial ethics provision for the crypto market structure bill underpinned the gains.
summary: Crypto markets experienced a rally due to speculation that President Trump agreed to a crucial ethics provision for a crypto market structure bill. This positive market movement was also associated with a progress report on "Clarity" and a rebound in Asian chip-stocks.

title: UK Parliament begins inquiry into banking chokepoint for crypto businesses
content: The U.K.’s Crypto and Digital Assets All-Party Parliamentary Group (APPG) is focused on banks that refused crypto firms accounts or introduced restrictions on crypto transactions.
summary: The UK Parliament's Crypto and Digital Assets All-Party Parliamentary Group (APPG) has launched an inquiry. This investigation targets banks that have refused to provide accounts to crypto firms or have imposed restrictions on crypto-related transactions. The move addresses a significant "chokepoint" issue faced by the cryptocurrency industry in the UK.

title: Inside Cardano's 'Van Rossum' hard fork, and what it means for users
content: The Van Rossem hard fork moved Cardano to version 11 on Saturday. And for the first time, Cardano's community voted in the upgrade, not the company that built it.
summary: Cardano successfully implemented its 'Van Rossum' hard fork on Saturday, upgrading the platform to version 11. This hard fork marks a significant milestone as it was the first time the Cardano community, rather than the developing company, voted on the upgrade.

title: Tom Lee's Bitmine slowed ether purchases as it bought back $86 million in stock
content: The company added just 7,430 ETH, worth about $14 million, last week as it nears its goal to corner 5% of Ethereum's supply.
summary: Tom Lee's Bitmine significantly slowed its Ethereum purchases last week, acquiring only 7,430 ETH worth $14 million. This reduction in crypto buying is linked to the company's decision to buy back $86 million in its own stock, even as it approaches its strategic goal of cornering 5% of Ethereum's total supply.

title: Saylor's Strategy raises cash reserves to $3.2 billion, leaving bitcoin holdings unchanged
content: For the second consecutive week, the firm kept bitcoin holdings steady, choosing to use the raised capital to boost cash for paying dividends on its preferred stock.
summary: Saylor's firm increased its cash reserves to $3.2 billion, utilizing raised capital to pay dividends on preferred stock. For the second consecutive week, the company maintained its bitcoin holdings without any changes.

title: Brazil’s securities regulator sets up task force with 60-day deadline for tokenization proposal
content: The framework will address critical issues like official ownership records, private key custody, transaction reversibility and system liability.
summary: Brazil's securities regulator has established a task force to develop a tokenization proposal within 60 days. This framework will tackle crucial issues such as official ownership records, private key custody, transaction reversibility, and system liability for tokenized assets.

title: A bitcoin 'volmageddon' may be brewing, key indicator suggests
content: Your day-ahead look for July 20, 2026
summary: The article's title suggests a potential 'volmageddon' for Bitcoin, indicating that a period of extreme volatility may be imminent. A key indicator is reportedly signaling this brewing event, which could significantly impact the cryptocurrency market. This news serves as a forward-looking alert for Bitcoin investors and market participants.

title: Bank of Korea prepares for live CBDC transactions with 9 banks in September
content: Major banking heavyweights join a massive blockchain network test that will allow retail consumers to trade tokenized won freely across commercial lines.
summary: The Bank of Korea is preparing for live central bank digital currency (CBDC) transactions with 9 banks in September, allowing retail consumers to trade tokenized won freely. This move is part of a massive blockchain network test. Major banking heavyweights are participating in the test.

title: Cross-chain protocol Allbridge halts after $1.65 million flash loan exploit
content: The attacker used a $1.12 million flash loan from Kamino to manipulate pool ratios, enabling them to withdraw assets at favorable rates before bridging funds.
summary: A cross-chain protocol called Allbridge was exploited for $1.65 million using a $1.12 million flash loan from Kamino, allowing the attacker to manipulate pool ratios and withdraw assets at favorable rates. The attacker bridged funds after the manipulation. The exploit has caused Allbridge to halt operations.

title: U.S. regulatory developments and earnings, ECB rate decision: Crypto Week Ahead
content: Your look at what's coming in the week starting July 20.
summary: The upcoming week will see significant events in the crypto space, including US regulatory developments and an ECB rate decision. These events may impact the cryptocurrency market. The week starting July 20 is expected to be notable for crypto enthusiasts.

title: BIS warns stablecoins could weaken capital controls in emerging markets
content: Researchers found dollar-backed stablecoins are less affected by capital controls than traditional bank deposits, raising new questions about monetary sovereignty in emerging markets.
summary: The Bank of International Settlements warns that stablecoins could weaken capital controls in emerging markets, as dollar-backed stablecoins are less affected by these controls than traditional bank deposits. This raises concerns about monetary sovereignty in these markets. The findings have implications for the global financial system.

title: CoinShares debuts Bitcoin mining ETF in Europe entrance
content: The UCITS ETF, CoinShares’ first in Europe, began trading on Deutsche Börse Xetra, tracking a rules-based index of publicly listed BTC miners.
summary: CoinShares has launched a Bitcoin mining ETF in Europe, listed on Deutsche Börse Xetra, which tracks a rules-based index of publicly listed BTC miners. This is the company's first ETF in Europe. The ETF provides exposure to the Bitcoin mining industry

title: More MiCA-licensed crypto firms may exit EU market: Gate Europe CEO
content: Gate Europe’s Giovanni Cunti says some MiCA-licensed crypto firms may struggle to sustain compliance costs as Europe enters its new regulatory era.
summary: Gate Europe's CEO, Giovanni Cunti, predicts that some MiCA-licensed crypto firms may exit the European market. This is due to the potential struggle to sustain compliance costs under Europe's new regulatory framework.

title: Russian parliament passes law regulating crypto market
content: The State Duma legislation creates a regulated framework for crypto market participants and now moves to President Vladimir Putin for his signature before becoming law.
summary: The Russian parliament has passed a law to regulate the crypto market, which will create a framework for participants and is awaiting President Vladimir Putin's signature. This law aims to provide a regulated environment for cryptocurrency activities. The legislation's approval marks a significant step towards formalizing Russia's crypto industry.

title: UK parliamentary group probes banking barriers for crypto firms
content: A UK parliamentary group launched an inquiry into banking restrictions on crypto firms and consumers, including their impact on investment and competition.
summary: A UK parliamentary group has launched an inquiry into the banking restrictions faced by cryptocurrency firms and consumers. This investigation aims to understand the impact of these barriers on investment and competition within the UK's crypto sector.

title: Base’s 1:1-backed tokenized equities launch ‘imminent,’ Pollak says
content: The Coinbase-backed Ethereum layer-2 is preparing to expand its financial offerings as it pivots away from its earlier social-first strategy.
summary: Coinbase-backed Ethereum layer-2 Base is imminently launching 1:1-backed tokenized equities. This strategic move signals a pivot away from its earlier social-first approach towards expanding its financial offerings within the crypto ecosystem.

title: Grayscale files S-1 for first US Worldcoin ETF
content: The filing expands Grayscale’s growing lineup of crypto-related exchange-traded products outside of Bitcoin and Ether.
summary: Grayscale has filed an S-1 for a Worldcoin ETF, expanding its lineup of crypto-related exchange-traded products beyond Bitcoin and Ether. This move indicates Grayscale's continued growth in the cryptocurrency market. The ETF is set to be the first of its kind in the US.

title: Celsius co-founders Leon, Goldstein to pay FTC over $6M
content: The settlements add to former Celsius CEO Alex Mashinsky’s $10 million FTC settlement in April.
summary: Celsius co-founders Leon and Goldstein have agreed to pay over $6M in settlements to the FTC, following former CEO Alex Mashinsky's $10 million settlement in April. This development is part of the ongoing regulatory actions against Celsius. The settlements highlight the ongoing scrutiny of cryptocurrency companies.

title: London Stock Exchange eyes overnight trading launch in 2027: FT
content: The move comes as traditional exchanges face growing competition from crypto markets and tokenized equity platforms that offer around-the-clock trading.
summary: The London Stock Exchange is considering launching overnight trading by 2027. This initiative is a direct response to increasing competition from crypto markets and tokenized equity platforms that offer continuous, around-the-clock trading.

title: Patrick Witt defers military training as CLARITY heads to Senate
content: The decision keeps the administration’s chief crypto policy negotiator in place during what could be a decisive few weeks for the CLARITY Act.
summary: Patrick Witt, the administration's chief crypto policy negotiator, has deferred military training to remain in place during a crucial period for the CLARITY Act. The CLARITY Act is heading to the Senate, and Witt's presence is significant for its negotiation. This decision allows Witt to continue his work on the Act.

title: Democrats added certain consumer protection rules to CLARITY: Coinbase exec
content: Coinbase vice chair Ryan VanGrack said that Democratic lawmakers had added provisions on customer protection to a digital asset market structure bill under consideration in the US Senate.
summary: Coinbase Vice Chair Ryan VanGrack announced that Democratic lawmakers have included customer protection provisions in a digital asset market structure bill currently under review in the US Senate. This indicates ongoing legislative efforts to establish regulations for the cryptocurrency industry.

title: Tether Gold recognized as Accepted Spot Commodity in Abu Dhabi financial center
content: The recognition allows regulated firms in Abu Dhabi Global Market to offer services involving Tether Gold as tokenized commodities gain broader adoption.
summary: Tether Gold has been officially recognized as an accepted spot commodity within the Abu Dhabi Global Market (ADGM). This recognition permits regulated firms in ADGM to offer services involving Tether Gold, indicating a growing institutional acceptance for tokenized commodities.

title: SEC sues Mining Automatic and founder over alleged $22M crypto mining scheme
content: The SEC alleges Mining Automatic and its founder raised $22 million from investors by promising guaranteed crypto mining returns while spending only a fraction of the funds on mining operations.
summary: The SEC has sued Mining Automatic and its founder over an alleged $22M crypto mining scheme, claiming they raised funds from investors with promises of guaranteed returns, but spent only a fraction on actual mining operations. The scheme allegedly deceived investors, and the SEC is taking action. This lawsuit highlights concerns over crypto investment scams.

title: Nigerian president signs order on approach to crypto regulation, taxes
content: The West African country’s executive order established a virtual asset council and addressed the fragmentation of crypto regulation for oversight and enforcement.
summary: The Nigerian president has signed an executive order establishing a virtual asset council to address and streamline crypto regulation, taxation, oversight, and enforcement within the West African country. This move aims to unify the country's approach to virtual assets.

title: Bernstein raises Robinhood price target, cites tokenization and prediction markets
content: The brokerage’s blockchain strategy, including tokenized equities and its Arbitrum-based Robinhood Chain, could reduce its reliance on traditional crypto trading, analysts said.
summary: Bernstein raised its price target for Robinhood, citing the brokerage's evolving blockchain strategy. This strategy includes tokenized equities and an Arbitrum-based Robinhood Chain, which analysts believe could reduce its reliance on traditional crypto trading while still leveraging the blockchain space.

title: IREN jumps 16% after raising AI cloud revenue target above $4B
content: The Bitcoin miner raised its year-end AI cloud revenue target to more than $4 billion after signing $2.8 billion in new contracts with AI developers.
summary: IREN, a Bitcoin miner, experienced a 16% stock surge after raising its year-end AI cloud revenue target to over $4 billion. This significant increase follows the signing of $2.8 billion in new contracts with AI developers, signaling a major expansion into the AI infrastructure market.

title: Coinbase says ‘low-risk’ config change behind 50-minute July 14 outage impacting trading, card transactions
content: Coinbase said user funds were never at risk, and user transactions that were stuck “in-flight” were completed once service was restored.
summary: Coinbase experienced a 50-minute outage on July 14, impacting trading and card transactions, but user funds were never at risk. The issue was caused by a 'low-risk' configuration change. All stuck transactions were completed once service was restored.

title: Telegram plans ‘largest rollout of a non-custodial crypto wallet in human history’
content: The messaging platform has long been viewed as a potential gateway for bringing a wider audience into crypto.
summary: Telegram plans to roll out a non-custodial crypto wallet, potentially bringing a wider audience into cryptocurrency. This move is seen as a significant step in increasing crypto adoption. The rollout is touted as the largest in human history.

title: Arcus rolls out 24/7 tokenized US stocks and perpetual markets on Robinhood Chain
content: Arcus rolled out 24/7 trading for over 95 Stock Tokens on Robinhood Chain and also introduced beta perpetual markets with USDG collateral.
summary: Arcus has launched 24/7 trading for over 95 tokenized US stocks on Robinhood Chain, and also introduced beta perpetual markets with USDG collateral. This allows for continuous trading beyond traditional market hours. The move aims to increase accessibility and flexibility for traders.

title: Aztec upgrades to V5 in alpha, adding full private execution environment to decentralized Ethereum L2
content: Aztec V5 focuses on supporting "client-side proving," enabling computation-heavy ZK proofs on simple devices like phones and laptops.
summary: Aztec has upgraded to V5 in alpha, introducing a full private execution environment to a decentralized Ethereum Layer 2 solution, focusing on client-side proving to enable complex zero-knowledge proofs on basic devices. This upgrade allows for computation-heavy ZK proofs to be performed on devices like phones and laptops. The development aims to enhance the overall performance and accessibility of the network.

title: London Stock Exchange plans overnight venue to extend trading hours: FT
content: London Stock Exchange plans an overnight venue as traditional exchanges extend trading hours amid competition from 24/7 crypto platforms.
summary: The London Stock Exchange (LSE) is planning to launch an overnight trading venue to extend its operational hours. This initiative is a strategic response to increasing competition from 24/7 cryptocurrency platforms, aiming to offer continuous market access.

title: UK parliamentary group launches probe into crypto sector’s banking challenges
content: This comes weeks after the publication of the UK’s new crypto regulatory framework, set to take effect in October 2027.
summary: A UK parliamentary group is investigating the banking challenges faced by the crypto sector, following the publication of the UK's new crypto regulatory framework, set to take effect in October 2027. The probe aims to address the issues crypto companies have in accessing banking services. This development is significant for the crypto industry in the UK.

title: SEC charges Florida man and company in alleged $22 million crypto mining fraud scheme
content: The defendants allegedly raised $22 million from more than 380 investors through a fraudulent crypto asset mining scheme.
summary: The SEC has charged a Florida man and his company with allegedly running a $22 million crypto mining fraud scheme, deceiving over 380 investors. The scheme involved false promises of crypto asset mining returns. The defendants are accused of raising funds through fraudulent means.

title: Trump agrees to ethics provision as crypto bill inches closer to Senate vote
content: President Trump has agreed to ethics language — potentially moving the ball forward to getting crypto legislation passed into law.
summary: President Trump has agreed to ethics language in a crypto bill, which may help move the legislation forward to a Senate vote. The bill's progress could potentially lead to the passage of cryptocurrency legislation into law. This development is a significant step for the crypto community.

title: Grayscale could take Worldcoin to Wall Street after ETF filing with the SEC
content: The first exchange-traded fund tied to Worldcoin could soon be headed to U.S. markets, as Grayscale files for a Worldcoin ETF.
summary: Grayscale has filed for a Worldcoin ETF with the SEC, which could lead to the first exchange-traded fund tied to Worldcoin being listed on US markets. This move could increase accessibility to Worldcoin for traditional investors. The ETF would track the performance of Worldcoin.

title: Bitcoin Price Closes in on $67,000, Lifting Strategy and Other Crypto Stocks
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

Bitcoin Price Closes in on $67,000, Lifting Strategy and Other Crypto Stocks
Bitcoin's price jumped Tuesday to nearly $67,000 per coin, bringing crypto stocks with it. 
This post Bitcoin Price Closes in on $67,000, Lifting Strategy and Other Crypto Stocks first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Mathew Di Salvo.
summary: Bitcoin's price surged to nearly $67,000 per coin, also lifting other crypto stocks. This increase indicates a positive trend in the cryptocurrency market. The rise in Bitcoin's price is likely to have a significant impact on the overall crypto market.

title: U.S. Treasury Freezes $130 Million Iran-Linked IRGC Crypto Wallet, Tracks Ayatollah’s Assets
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

U.S. Treasury Freezes $130 Million Iran-Linked IRGC Crypto Wallet, Tracks Ayatollah’s Assets
Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said the U.S. froze a $130 million crypto wallet linked to Iran's IRGC as part of a broader effort to track and seize assets tied to Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei and increase economic pressure on Tehran.
This post U.S. Treasury Freezes $130 Million Iran-Linked IRGC Crypto Wallet, Tracks Ayatollah’s Assets first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.
summary: The U.S. Treasury has frozen a $130 million crypto wallet linked to Iran's IRGC, as part of a broader effort to track and seize assets tied to Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei. This move aims to increase economic pressure on Tehran. The action is part of a larger campaign to target Iranian assets.

title: Russia Passes Landmark Crypto Law, Setting State-Run Rails for Sanctioned Trade
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

Russia Passes Landmark Crypto Law, Setting State-Run Rails for Sanctioned Trade
Russia's parliament passed its first comprehensive crypto law, creating a state-regulated framework for exchanges and investors.
This post Russia Passes Landmark Crypto Law, Setting State-Run Rails for Sanctioned Trade first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.
summary: Russia's parliament has passed a landmark crypto law, creating a state-regulated framework for exchanges and investors, marking a significant step in the country's approach to cryptocurrency. This law sets the stage for state-run rails for sanctioned trade. The move is expected to have implications for the global crypto market.

title: Galaxy (GLXY) Commits $5 Million to Prepare Bitcoin for the Quantum Threat
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

Galaxy (GLXY) Commits $5 Million to Prepare Bitcoin for the Quantum Threat
Galaxy Digital launched a Bitcoin Quantum Readiness Initiative to help prepare the Bitcoin network for the potential threat posed by powerful quantum computers.
This post Galaxy (GLXY) Commits $5 Million to Prepare Bitcoin for the Quantum Threat first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.
summary: Galaxy Digital has committed $5 million to prepare the Bitcoin network for potential threats from quantum computers through its Bitcoin Quantum Readiness Initiative. This initiative aims to address the risk that powerful quantum computers could pose to the security of the Bitcoin network. The move highlights growing concerns about the impact of quantum computing on cryptocurrency security.

title: Bitcoin Miner Hut 8 Shares Jump on $9.8 Billion AI Data Center Deal
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

Bitcoin Miner Hut 8 Shares Jump on $9.8 Billion AI Data Center Deal
Bitcoin miner Hut 8 has said it signed a second 15-year lease ​worth $9.8 billion for its AI data center. 
This post Bitcoin Miner Hut 8 Shares Jump on $9.8 Billion AI Data Center Deal first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Mathew Di Salvo.
summary: Hut 8, a Bitcoin miner, has signed a 15-year lease worth $9.8 billion for its AI data center, causing its shares to jump. The deal is significant for the company and its operations. This news indicates a growing intersection between cryptocurrency and artificial intelligence.

title: Coinbase Executive Says Clarity Act Has ‘Tremendous Momentum’ in the Senate
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

Coinbase Executive Says Clarity Act Has ‘Tremendous Momentum’ in the Senate
Coinbase's Ryan VanGrack said the Clarity Act is gaining strong Senate momentum and argued it would bring long-awaited federal rules, stronger consumer protections, and regulatory clarity to the crypto industry.
This post Coinbase Executive Says Clarity Act Has ‘Tremendous Momentum’ in the Senate first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.
summary: A Coinbase executive reported that the Clarity Act is gaining momentum in the Senate, which could bring federal rules and regulatory clarity to the crypto industry. The act is expected to provide stronger consumer protections. This development is seen as a positive step for the crypto community.

title: Russia Moves to Rein In Crypto Fraud With New Legislation
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

Russia Moves to Rein In Crypto Fraud With New Legislation
Russia is pushing ahead with regulating the cryptocurrency market, with the State Duma considering a bill on combating fraud in the industry, according to reports.  The bill will look at “combating the illegal use of cryptocurrencies within our country,” Anatoly Aksakov, chairman of the State Duma Committee on Financial Markets, reportedly said. Lawmakers will vote […]
This post Russia Moves to Rein In Crypto Fraud With New Legislation first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Mathew Di Salvo.
summary: Russia is considering a bill to combat crypto fraud, aiming to regulate the cryptocurrency market and prevent illegal use of cryptocurrencies within the country. The bill is being reviewed by the State Duma and will be put to a vote. This move is part of Russia's efforts to rein in crypto fraud and establish clearer guidelines for the industry.

title: The Download: AI hiring biases, and weather data sabotage
content: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. AI is more likely than humans to form biases when hiring The next time you apply for a job, AI may screen your résumé before any human sees it. But there’s…
summary: A study finds that AI is more likely to form biases when hiring than humans, which can affect the job screening process. This discovery highlights a significant issue in the use of AI in hiring. The finding has implications for the fairness of AI-powered hiring tools.

title: AI is more likely than humans to form biases when hiring
content: The next time you apply for a job, AI may screen your résumé before any human sees it. But there’s good reason to question whether AI will judge you fairly. Researchers already know that LLMs pick up human biases from their training data. New research suggests that LLMs can also develop their own biases from…
summary: Researchers have found that AI, specifically large language models (LLMs), can develop biases when screening job applicants, potentially leading to unfair hiring practices. This is because LLMs can pick up human biases from their training data and also create their own biases. As a result, AI may be more likely to form biases than humans when hiring.

title: The Download: OpenAI unveils GPT-Red and heat pumps rise in the US
content: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Meet GPT-Red: an LLM super-hacker OpenAI built to make its models safer OpenAI has built an LLM super-hacker called GPT-Red that it uses as a sparring partner to help its other…
summary: OpenAI has developed GPT-Red, a large language model designed to test and improve the safety of its other models by acting as a sparring partner. This innovation aims to enhance the security and reliability of OpenAI's technologies. GPT-Red is used to simulate hacking attempts and identify potential vulnerabilities.

title: US threatens sanctions against Chinese AI models over IP theft
content: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said the U.S. could sanction Chinese open AI models over alleged IP theft, expanding the Trump administration's campaign to slow China's AI advances.
summary: The US is considering sanctions against Chinese open AI models due to alleged IP theft, aiming to slow China's AI advancements. This move expands the Trump administration's campaign against China's AI development. The potential sanctions could impact the global AI landscape.

title: Music streamer Deezer says more than 50% of daily uploads are AI-generated
content: Deezer said more than 90,000 AI-generated tracks were uploaded daily on the platform in June.
summary: Music streaming platform Deezer reports that over 50% of its daily uploads are AI-generated, with more than 90,000 AI-generated tracks uploaded daily in June. This significant milestone highlights the growing presence of AI-generated music. The platform's data underscores the increasing role of artificial intelligence in music creation.

title: Anthropic’s landmark $1.5B copyright settlement is approved
content: The final approval settles one case, but it doesn't resolve the broader issue of using copyrighted works to train AI models.
summary: A $1.5B copyright settlement between Anthropic and a party involved in a case about using copyrighted works to train AI models has been approved. This settles one case, but the broader issue remains unresolved. The settlement's impact on the AI community is significant.

title: Google is working on a new AI chip designed to make Gemini more efficient
content: Alphabet, Google's parent company, is reportedly working on a new chip designed to make its Gemini models run much more efficiently.
summary: Google's parent company Alphabet is developing a new AI chip to improve the efficiency of its Gemini models. This chip aims to enhance performance while reducing resource usage. The development is part of Google's ongoing efforts to advance its AI capabilities.

title: AI’s most important protocol is getting a little bit easier to use
content: Under the new system, the protocol will take a looser, "stateless" approach to session IDs on the server side, similar to how most ordinary websites already work.
summary: The Model Context Protocol (MCP) is being improved for easier use, it's a fundamental protocol for AI interoperability, enabling secure access to external data sources and services. This upgrade facilitates smoother interactions between AI models and external tools. MCP acts as a standard interface, reducing the need for custom engineering

title: OpenAI is scared of open-weight models. Should the US be?
content: Talk of banning Chinese-made open-weight LLMs reveals the challenge of  turning AI into a business.
summary: OpenAI is concerned about open-weight models, particularly those made in China, which poses a challenge in turning AI into a business. The US is considering banning these models, highlighting the complexities of AI development and regulation. This issue reveals the tension between open-source AI models and commercialization.

title: Can an Apple lawsuit derail OpenAI’s hardware plans?
content: On the latest episode of Equity, we debate whether Apple's lawsuit will cast a shadow over OpenAi's much-discussed plans to get into hardware and go public.
summary: A lawsuit from Apple may impact OpenAI's plans to enter the hardware market and go public, according to a recent episode of Equity. The lawsuit's outcome could have significant implications for OpenAI's future. The discussion on Equity explores the potential effects on OpenAI's hardware ambitions.

title: ‘Odyssey’ director Christopher Nolan calls AI an obvious ‘Trojan horse’
content: "Everybody knows the Greeks are inside."
summary: Director Christopher Nolan has publicly voiced his concern regarding artificial intelligence, labeling it an 'obvious Trojan horse'. He suggested that the inherent risks or hidden intentions of AI are apparent, stating that 'everybody knows the Greeks are inside'.

title: Nonprofit Current AI is racing to build the World Wide Web of AI, free for all
content: Current AI, a nonprofit building AI that leaves no one culture behind, has made remarkable progress across devices, AI chat, and more.
summary: Current AI, a non-profit organization, is actively working to create a 'World Wide Web of AI' that is free and accessible to everyone, ensuring no culture is left behind. They have reported significant progress in areas such as AI chat and device compatibility.

title: Kimi: Threat or menace?
content: Chinese company Moonshot AI released a new version of its Kimi model this week, prompting concern about "full AI communism."
summary: Chinese company Moonshot AI has released a new version of its Kimi model, sparking concerns about the potential implications of advanced AI. The concept of 'full AI communism' has been raised, implying a significant shift in the way AI systems are developed and utilized. This development has notable implications for the AI community.

title: Databricks hits $188B valuation, extending its run as AI’s favorite second act
content: Databricks has remade its image into an AI company and has published research on the cost savings of open-weight AI models for coding.
summary: Databricks has transformed into an AI company and published research on the cost savings of open weight AI models for coding, achieving a valuation of $188B. This development solidifies its position in the AI industry. The company's focus on AI has led to significant growth and recognition.

title: Google launches a cheaper alternative to large AI security models like Mythos
content:
Google is launching Gemini 3.6 Flash alongside a new security model dedicated to quickly finding and patching security vulnerabilities. In a blog post on Tuesday, Google describes Gemini 3.5 Flash Cyber as a "cost-efficient and highly capable alternative" to larger, more expensive AI systems, such as the one offered by Anthropic's Mythos.
The cybersecurity model is built upon Gemini 3.5 Flash and will be available first to governments and trusted partners via CodeMender, Google's security-focused coding agent. As noted by Google, CodeMender can call upon 3.5 Flash Cyber "multiple times at high speed and low cost," allowing the AI agents to  …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: Google has launched Gemini 3.6 Flash, a cost-efficient alternative to large AI security models, along with a new security model to quickly find and patch vulnerabilities. The model is built on Gemini 3.5 Flash and will be available to governments and trusted partners. This aims to provide a cheaper option to expensive AI systems like Anthropic's Mythos.

title: America needs to stop getting shocked by Chinese AI
content:
People visit the booth of Kimi, an LLM developed by the Chinese startup Moonshot, during the World AI Conference in Shanghai, China, Monday, July 20, 2026. | Image: LONG WEI/ Feature China/Future Publishing via Getty Images	

Last week, two Chinese AI companies unveiled models they say can credibly compete with the best systems from OpenAI and Anthropic. The response was swift and predictable. Markets wobbled, commentators declared Silicon Valley shooketh, and policymakers reached for the familiar language of arms races and wake-up calls. 
In one headline, The Associated Press said a Chinese model had taken the "US tech industry by surprise." Bloomberg described it as a "surprise breakthrough" that is "roiling markets" and sending global tech stocks tumbling over concerns it could force US firms to rethink their gargantuan spending on data centers, chips, and ot …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: Two Chinese AI companies have unveiled models that can compete with the best systems from OpenAI and Anthropic, causing market wobbles and concerns about the US tech industry. The development has been described as a surprise breakthrough that could force US firms to rethink their spending. The news has sparked discussions about an AI arms race between China and the US.

title: Here are the 30,000 songs Sony is suing Udio’s AI music generator over
content:
Sony Music Entertainment has filed another lawsuit against Udio, accusing the AI music generator of infringing the copyright of more than 30,000 of its songs, ranging from Elvis Presley's Hound Dog to Beyoncé's Say My Name, and Harry Styles' As It Was. The lawsuit, filed in a New York court on Monday, claims that this list represents "only a small portion of Plaintiffs' works that Udio infringed," as reported earlier by Music Business Worldwide.
In 2024, Sony, along with Universal Music Group and Warner Records, filed a lawsuit against Udio and the AI music-maker Suno. After gaining access to Udio's training data through discovery, Sony cla …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: Sony Music Entertainment has filed a lawsuit against Udio, an AI music generator, for copyright infringement of over 30,000 songs, including works by Elvis Presley, Beyoncé, and Harry Styles. The lawsuit claims this is only a small portion of the infringed works. Sony had previously filed a lawsuit against Udio and another AI music-maker in 2024.

title: Adobe’s ‘natural look’ camera app embraces generative AI
content:
Welcome to the “what is a photo” debate, Adobe. | Image: Adobe	

Adobe's experimental camera app has taken an unexpected turn. After Project Indigo was launched last year to provide a "more natural (SLR-like) look" for iPhone photography, the Indigo camera app is now being updated with a suite of generative AI tools. And the change doesn't rely upon Adobe's own Firefly AI models.
Adobe describes the new "AI Playground" tooling suite as an experiment and says there's a button that allows users to opt out and continue using the app as before. Free access to the suite with no sign-on requirement is being tested with a small percentage of Indigo users over the next few weeks. "Our goal is to learn how people …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: Adobe's Indigo camera app is being updated with generative AI tools, allowing users to opt out and continue using the app as before. The update is currently being tested with a small percentage of users. The goal is to learn how people use the new AI features.

title: China delivers a one-two punch to America’s AI dominance
content:
China's leading AI companies are ramping up the pressure on Silicon Valley, as Moonshot and Alibaba unveiled models they claim can go toe-to-toe with the best from OpenAI and Anthropic at a fraction of the cost. The rapid-fire releases suggest America's lead at the AI frontier is increasingly tight, just as the technology is becoming central to national security, economic power, and geopolitical influence. 
The opening salvo came from Beijing-based Moonshot AI, one of China's leading AI model developers, which unveiled Kimi K3 on Friday. Moonshot claims its own testing ranks it consistently above nearly every US system, trailing only OpenAI …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: China's AI companies, such as Moonshot and Alibaba, are releasing models that can compete with top US models at a lower cost, challenging America's lead in AI. This development has significant implications for national security, economic power, and geopolitical influence. China's rapid progress in AI may erode the US's dominance in the field.

title: Beyond grep: The case for a context-rich AI coding harness
content: Augment Code's Vinay Perneti talks models, harnesses, and context.
summary: Vinay Perneti from Augment Code discusses the potential of AI in coding, suggesting a context-rich AI coding harness that goes beyond traditional tools like grep. This approach aims to improve coding efficiency and accuracy. The conversation involves models and harnesses in the context of AI-assisted coding.

title: Will AI fix prior authorization—or make it worse?
content: The government is piloting a program that uses AI for insurance-coverage decisions.
summary: The government is piloting a program that leverages AI for insurance-coverage decisions, particularly concerning prior authorization. This initiative raises questions about whether AI will improve the efficiency of these processes or introduce new challenges.

title: It's official: EU will force Google to share search data and open up AI on Android
content: Google says these changes could endanger user privacy and security.
summary: The EU will force Google to share search data and open up AI on Android, which Google claims could compromise user privacy and security. This move is part of the EU's efforts to regulate big tech companies. The changes aim to promote competition and transparency in the tech industry.

title: xAI can’t deny Grok makes CSAM anymore. So it’s suing users.
content: Elon Musk's xAI files first lawsuit against Grok user accused of making child sex images.
summary: Elon Musk's xAI is suing a user of its Grok platform for allegedly creating child sex images, marking the company's first lawsuit. This comes after xAI claimed it could no longer deny that Grok was being used for this purpose. The lawsuit highlights the challenges faced by AI companies in regulating user-generated content.

title: Fear of humanoid robots spurs human workers to strike at Hyundai auto factory
content: Hyundai aims to deploy 25,000 Atlas robots starting with US factories in 2028.
summary: Hyundai plans to deploy 25,000 Atlas robots in its factories, starting with US locations in 2028, prompting human workers to strike due to fears of job displacement by humanoid robots. The strike highlights concerns about the impact of automation on employment. The introduction of Atlas robots is expected to significantly change the factory workforce.

title: Linus Torvalds to critics of AI coding in Linux: "Fork it. Or just walk away."
content: Creator says he will "very loudly ignore" those arguing for a ban on AI tools.
summary: Linus Torvalds, the creator of Linux, has responded to critics of AI coding in Linux, stating that he will ignore those arguing for a ban on AI tools and telling them to fork the project or walk away. This response indicates a strong stance in favor of AI integration in Linux. The decision may have significant implications for the future of Linux development.

title: Lawsuit claims Meta's layoff decisions were made by AI, not humans
content: Meta denies using AI to terminate workers with disabilities and medical problems.
summary: A lawsuit claims that Meta used AI to make layoff decisions, potentially targeting workers with disabilities and medical problems. Meta denies the allegations, stating that humans made the termination decisions. The lawsuit raises concerns about the use of AI in employment decisions.

title: New York bans data center construction for a year, rattling AI industry
content: New York’s data center moratorium may become the blueprint for anti-AI movement.
summary: New York has implemented a one-year ban on data center construction, which may have significant implications for the AI industry and could potentially serve as a model for an anti-AI movement. This moratorium may hinder the growth and development of AI technologies that rely on data centers. The ban's impact on the AI industry is likely to be closely watched by experts and stakeholders.

title: Apple sues OpenAI after ex-engineer allegedly used bug to steal trade secrets
content: OpenAI accused of conspiring with former Apple employees to steal trade secrets.
summary: Apple is suing OpenAI, alleging that a former Apple engineer used a bug to steal trade secrets and conspired with OpenAI. The engineer allegedly used the stolen information to benefit OpenAI. The lawsuit claims a breach of contract and misappropriation of trade secrets.

title: Now, defenders are embracing the prompt injection, too
content: "Context bombing" tricks hacking agents into shutting down before they can do harm.
summary: Defenders are using a technique called 'context bombing' or 'prompt injection' to trick hacking agents into shutting down before they can cause harm. This method involves providing misleading context to the hacking agents. The technique is showing promise in preventing cyber attacks.

title: Simulating everything, sort of: The promise and limits of world models
content: Experts explain how they work, what they can do, and what's still unsettled.
summary: Experts discuss the capabilities and limitations of world models, which are simulating complex systems. These models can mimic various aspects of reality, but their potential and current state are still being explored. World models have significant implications for fields like artificial intelligence.

title: OpenAI wants its new tool to do your work for you and with you
content: Rebranded Codex promises independent workflows that can run "for hours if needed."
summary: OpenAI's new tool, rebranded Codex, aims to assist and automate workflows, allowing for independent operation for extended periods. This advancement has the potential to significantly impact productivity. Codex can run for hours if needed, making it a valuable asset for various tasks.

title: Jack Mallers steps down as XXI Capital CEO as Tether's plans to merge three bitcoin firms falls
content: The proposed three-way merger between Twenty One Capital, Mallers' Strike, and Elektron Energy has been abandoned.
summary: The proposed three-way merger between Twenty One Capital, Jack Mallers' Strike, and Elektron Energy has been abandoned. This development coincides with Jack Mallers stepping down as CEO of XXI Capital.

title: Live markets: Bitcoin rises to five-week high near $67,000 on boosted hope for Clarity Act
content: Bitcoin ETFs have pulled in roughly $727 million over the past week, the most sustained stretch of buying since the record outflows of June.
summary: Bitcoin ETFs have recorded a fifth consecutive day of inflows, marking the most sustained stretch of buying since April. This positive run has attracted approximately $727 million, signaling a significant shift after record outflows in June.

title: Bitcoin hits one-month high above $66,000 as the chip trade turns back into a tailwind
content: Asian semiconductor shares rebounded hard, a five-day ETF inflow streak passed $600 million, and oil pulled back on Middle East diplomacy.
summary: Bitcoin reached a two-week high near $65,500, supported by a significant rebound in Asian semiconductor shares. This price increase was further bolstered by a five-day streak of ETF inflows exceeding $600 million and a decline in oil prices.

content: This restructuring is part of a strategy to build a full-stack payments platform following the company's acquisitions of Monavate and Baanx.
summary: Exodus is set to reduce its global workforce by 25% as part of a strategic restructuring to develop a full-stack payments platform. This move follows the company's recent acquisitions of Monavate and Baanx.

title: Bitcoin ETFs post 5-day inflow streak, longest since May
content: US spot Bitcoin ETFs recorded $227 million in inflows as BTC climbed above $65,000, extending their longest winning streak since early May.
summary: US spot Bitcoin ETFs recorded $227 million in inflows, marking their longest positive streak since early May. This financial activity occurred as Bitcoin's price surpassed the $65,000 mark.

title: Bitcoin defies recent tech stocks sell-off: Are bulls eyeing $70K rally?
content: Despite muted derivatives sentiment, Bitcoin’s decoupling and Strategy’s cash raise set the stage for a potential rally to $70,000.
summary: Bitcoin is showing signs of a potential rally towards $70,000, defying a general sell-off in tech stocks and quiet derivatives markets. This optimism is fueled by Bitcoin's decoupling from broader tech trends and a recent cash raise by Strategy.

title: Exodus to cut 25% of staff in company reorganization
content: The wallet company said it expected the layoffs to generate between $10 million and $13 million in savings as part of its strategy to build a full-stack card issuance and payments platform.

These layoffs are anticipated to generate $10 million to $13 million in savings, supporting the company's strategic shift to build a full-stack card issuance and payments platform.

title: CoinShares launches UCITS platform targeting Europe’s institutional investors

summary: CoinShares is expanding into Europe's $30 trillion UCITS ecosystem by launching a new platform. This initiative includes a Bitcoin Mining ETF, targeting institutional investors across the continent.

title: Advancing next-gen AI with materials science innovation
content: The conversation about AI often centers on algorithms, computing power, or huge investments in new semiconductor fabrication plants and hyperscale data centers. But beneath each of these advances is another layer of innovation that makes them possible: advanced materials. Every new generation of AI technology demands more processing power, more memory, greater energy efficiency, and…
summary: The article highlights the critical, often overlooked role of advanced materials science in enabling next-generation AI technologies. It argues that while discussions frequently center on algorithms and computing power, innovations in materials are fundamental to meeting the increasing demands for processing power, memory, and energy efficiency.

title: Gritt exits stealth with $32 million for robots to build solar plants — then, everything else
content: Gritt is coming out of stealth with $34 million and plans to automate the hardest tasks on construction sites.
summary: Gritt has officially exited stealth mode, securing $34 million in funding. The company aims to automate the most challenging tasks on construction sites using robots, initially focusing on building solar plants and eventually expanding into other construction sectors.

title: Neil Rimer thinks the AI money is coming back out
content: Neil Rimer, the venture capitalist who co-founded Index Ventures, predicts the historic wealth AI is generating in Silicon Valley will have to be redistributed, voluntarily or involuntarily.
summary: Venture capitalist Neil Rimer, co-founder of Index Ventures, predicts that the significant wealth currently being generated by the artificial intelligence sector in Silicon Valley will eventually need to be redistributed. He suggests this redistribution of AI-derived wealth could occur either voluntarily or involuntarily.

title: I hate that I don’t hate this song made with Suno
content:
1010Benja won’t apologize for using AI. | Image: 1010Benja / Instagram	

I would never go so far as to say there's no place for AI in music (I'm a fan of Holly Herndon, after all). But I generally find music made with generative AI to be offensively boring, especially the outputs of Suno. So I'm having a bit of a tough time processing the fact that I actually quite enjoy 1010Benja's "Semiramis' Dream."
Benja has been unapologetic about his use of generative AI on his latest EP, Time Has Nothing To Do With What You Choose… The other three tracks can't quite hold a candle to what you find on 2024's Ten Total. But the opener "Semiramis' Dream" is infectious. It explodes out of the speakers with a jungle beat and Be …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: The author, usually critical of generative AI music, expresses surprise and enjoyment for 1010Benja's song "Semiramis' Dream," which was created using AI, specifically Suno. 1010Benja has been open about using generative AI in his latest EP, and this particular track challenges the author's general negative perception of AI-produced music.

title: Dave Eggers told OpenAI staff  that ChatGPT was ‘silencing an entire generation’
content:
Dave Eggers attends the "The Turning Point: To Be Destroyed" premiere. | Image: John Lamparski/Getty Images for Tribeca Festival	

Last year, Sam Altman invited author Dave Eggers to give a talk to around 200 OpenAI staffers. The man has written countless novels, screenplays, pieces of journalism, started McSweeney's, and founded multiple schools and nonprofits that support writers and the arts more broadly. So one might expect he'd roll into the company's offices and offer tips on being relentlessly prolific, or how to excel in multiple fields. Instead, he apparently laced into the company. According to the Financial Times, Eggers told the staff:
"The effect of ChatGPT on educators' lives is catastrophic. Whether you intended to do it or not, you've made every teache …

Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: Author Dave Eggers, invited by Sam Altman, criticized ChatGPT during a talk to OpenAI staff, stating its impact on educators is "catastrophic." He reportedly argued that the AI tool is "silencing an entire generation," challenging the company despite being expected to offer advice on productivity.

title: MoneyGram's CEO says blockchain works best when customers don't know it's there
content: In an interview with CoinDesk, MoneyGram CEO Anthony Soohoo said that their blockchain strategy has evolved from early experimentation into a broader effort to modernize the company's global payments infrastructure.
summary: MoneyGram's CEO, Anthony Soohoo, stated that the company's blockchain strategy has shifted from experimentation to modernizing its global payments infrastructure, with a focus on seamless integration. The goal is to utilize blockchain without the customer being aware of its presence. This approach aims to improve the overall payment experience.

title: Crypto market slips even as equities advance. Pump surges on social-media chatter
content: With a Fear and Greed index pointing solidly at "fear," crypto prices drifted lower even as stock index futures rose. PUMP's 20% surge was the only real headline in an otherwise quiet session.
summary: Crypto prices fell despite rising stock index futures, with the Fear and Greed index indicating fear, while PUMP surged 20% due to social media chatter. The crypto market's decline occurred in an otherwise quiet session. The surge in PUMP was the notable event in the market.

title: Hyperliquid plans to add decentralized prediction markets in upgrade to HIP-4
content: Permissionless prediction markets will become available first on testnet and later on mainnet, Hyperliquid said.
summary: Hyperliquid plans to introduce decentralized prediction markets as part of its HIP-4 upgrade, with the new feature initially available on testnet before being rolled out to mainnet. This upgrade aims to enhance the platform's capabilities. The introduction of permissionless prediction markets is expected to increase user engagement and participation.

title: MEXC adds Bittensor TAO staking for its global user base
content: The integration with validator Yuma gives millions of MEXC users access to Bittensor, an AI-focused blockchain whose ecosystem now spans 128 specialized subnets.
summary: MEXC has integrated Bittensor TAO staking, giving its users access to an AI-focused blockchain with 128 specialized subnets. This integration is made possible through validator Yuma. The move expands MEXC users' access to AI-focused blockchain technology.

title: Bitcoin nears seven-week high as stocks ignore Iran strikes, Trump 10% tariff plans
content: Bitcoin and crypto joined US stocks in brushing off US-Iran war escalation and the threat of fresh trade tariffs before the end of the month.
summary: Bitcoin and other cryptocurrencies have risen, along with US stocks, despite increased tensions between the US and Iran and potential new trade tariffs. The price of Bitcoin is nearing a seven-week high. This increase suggests that investors are currently viewing Bitcoin as a stable asset.

title: Robinhood-backed DEX Arcus expands with tokenized assets, perps
content: Arcus, built by the dYdX team, adds tokenized stocks and perpetual futures on Robinhood Chain as platforms compete to bring traditional assets onchain.
summary: Arcus, a decentralized exchange developed by the dYdX team, is expanding its offerings on the Robinhood Chain. The platform now includes tokenized stocks and perpetual futures, as it competes to integrate traditional financial assets onto the blockchain.

title: Tether-backed Twenty One, Strike merger plan scrapped: Bloomberg
content: Strike will remain a standalone company after the proposed three-way merger was scrapped, while Twenty One Capital and Elektron continue discussions, Bloomberg reported.
summary: A proposed three-way merger between Strike, Twenty One Capital, and Elektron has been scrapped, with Strike opting to remain a standalone company. Bloomberg reported that Twenty One Capital and Elektron are continuing their own discussions. This development impacts the cryptocurrency sector, particularly as Twenty One Capital is Tether-backed.

title: Binance & OKX users face $1,900 fines in Vietnam, Coinbase in China? Asia Express
content: Retail users in Vietnam who trade on offshore crypto exchanges like Binance or OKX face heavy penalties. Network School controversy in Malaysia over Israeli citizen ban.
summary: Retail users in Vietnam are facing fines of $1900 for trading on offshore crypto exchanges like Binance and OKX. This crackdown is part of a larger trend of regulation in Asia. The news also mentions a controversy in Malaysia related to a ban on Israeli citizens.

title: Peter Brandt predicts the exact day Bitcoin’s bear market will be over
content: Renowned trading analyst Peter Brandt says investing in Bitcoin today, will pay off much better in two to three years than buying AI stocks at current prices.
summary: Peter Brandt predicts the end of Bitcoin's bear market and suggests investing in Bitcoin will be more profitable than AI stocks in the long run. He believes returns will be higher in two to three years. This prediction may influence investment decisions in the crypto and AI spaces.

title: Bitget plans US expansion ‘with or without’ Clarity Act, CEO says
content: Bitget CEO Gracy Chen is leading the crypto exchange's push into the U.S. after initially shelving expansion plans in 2022.
summary: Bitget, a crypto exchange, is planning to expand into the US, led by CEO Gracy Chen, despite initial plans being shelved in 2022. The expansion will proceed with or without the Clarity Act. This move indicates the company's commitment to entering the US market.

title: Uphold launches fractional share trading in US for more than 4,000 stocks and ETFs
content: “Uphold customers can now sell Bitcoin to buy Berkshire Hathaway shares in a single step on the app," the company's U.S. president said.
summary: Uphold has launched fractional share trading for over 4,000 stocks and ETFs in the US, allowing users to invest in companies like Berkshire Hathaway. This service enables customers to sell Bitcoin and buy stocks in a single step on the app. The move expands Uphold's investment offerings beyond cryptocurrency.

title: London Stock Exchange’s LSE 24 signals a bet on digital, AI-driven markets: TD Cowen
content: London Stock Exchange will launch LSE 24, a 24/5 venue trading 17:00–07:50, with testing by end-2026 and ETPs first in H1 2027.
summary: The London Stock Exchange is launching LSE 24, a 24/5 trading venue that will utilize AI-driven markets, with testing set to begin by the end of 2026 and exchange-traded products to follow in the first half of 2027. This move signals the exchange's bet on digital markets. The launch will start with a limited schedule and expand in the future.

title: Malaysia shuts down Balaji Srinivasan’s Network School in Forest City
content: Malaysia revoked Network School's license, ordering the Balaji Srinivasan-founded digital nomad commune to cease operations July 22.
summary: Malaysia has shut down Network School, a digital nomad commune founded by Balaji Srinivasan, by revoking its license. The school was ordered to cease operations on July 22. This move may impact the community of remote workers and entrepreneurs associated with the school.

title: Jack Mallers leaves Twenty One as Strike exits Tether’s three-way bitcoin merger
content: Twenty One Capital named Raphael Zagury CEO, replacing founder Jack Mallers, as Strike exits a Tether-backed three-way merger.
summary: Jack Mallers has left his role as CEO of Twenty One Capital, with Raphael Zagury taking his place. The change comes as Strike exits a merger with Tether, a major player in the cryptocurrency space. This move may have significant implications for the future of cryptocurrency adoption and development.

title: Jito rolls out JTX self-custodial trading platform for Solana tokens and RWAs
content: Jito Labs launched JTX, a self-custodial Solana trading platform with spot trading for tokens and tokenized RWAs.
summary: Jito Labs has launched JTX, a self-custodial trading platform on Solana, offering spot trading for tokens and tokenized real-world assets. This platform allows users to maintain control over their assets while trading. JTX aims to provide a secure and transparent trading experience.

title: Morpho launches fixed-rate lending protocol Midnight on Base to expand onchain credit markets
content: Morpho has launched Midnight, a fixed-rate, fixed-term lending protocol on Base that complements its existing variable-rate lending protocol.
summary: Morpho has launched Midnight, a fixed-rate lending protocol on Base, to expand on-chain credit markets. This complements its existing variable-rate lending protocol. The launch aims to provide more options for lenders and borrowers in the crypto space.

title: Robinhood Chain tops $430 million in TVL, FalconX sees RWAs as the key differentiator
content: Robinhood Chain hit $431M TVL and 250K+ daily users in under three weeks, but memecoins account for 80% of its $9B DEX volume, FalconX says.
summary: Robinhood Chain has reached $431 million in total value locked and over 250,000 daily users in under three weeks, with a significant portion of its volume coming from memecoins. FalconX notes that real-world assets will be a key differentiator. The chain's rapid growth is noteworthy, but its reliance on memecoins may be a concern.

title: Kalshi, Polymarket open interest falls 20% as World Cup frenzy winds down
content: Sports accounted for 80% of both Kalshi and Polymarket's total volumes throughout the weekslong World Cup tournament.
summary: Kalshi and Polymarket's open interest fell by 20% as the World Cup frenzy subsided, with sports accounting for 80% of their total volumes during the tournament. The decline in interest is expected as the World Cup has concluded. This decrease may impact the platforms' activity levels.

title: Jack Mallers Steps Down as CEO of Bitcoin Treasury Twenty One Capital
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

Jack Mallers Steps Down as CEO of Bitcoin Treasury Twenty One Capital 
Jack Mallers has stepped down as CEO of bitcoin treasury Twenty One.

title: Bitcoin-Treasury Capital B Plans 10-for-1 Reverse Stock Split for September
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

Bitcoin-Treasury Capital B Plans 10-for-1 Reverse Stock Split for September
Capital B will execute a 10-for-1 reverse stock split in September as the Paris-listed bitcoin treasury company looks to attract more institutional investors while continuing to build its 3,139 BTC reserve.
This post Bitcoin-Treasury Capital B Plans 10-for-1 Reverse Stock Split for September first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.
summary: Capital B, a Paris-listed bitcoin treasury company, plans to execute a 10-for-1 reverse stock split in September to attract more institutional investors. The company currently holds 3,139 BTC in reserve. This move aims to boost the company's appeal to investors.

title: New Onramp Report Makes the Case for Spot Bitcoin Over Paper Claims as Price Sits at Half Its Peak
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

New Onramp Report Makes the Case for Spot Bitcoin Over Paper Claims as Price Sits at Half Its Peak
Onramp argues that spot bitcoin offers stronger long-term value than paper bitcoin exposure, highlighting bitcoin's fixed supply as its price trades roughly 50% below its all-time high.
This post New Onramp Report Makes the Case for Spot Bitcoin Over Paper Claims as Price Sits at Half Its Peak first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.
summary: Onramp released a report arguing that spot bitcoin has stronger long-term value than paper bitcoin exposure, citing its fixed supply as a key factor, as the price of bitcoin trades at roughly half its all-time high. The report highlights the benefits of investing in spot bitcoin. This comes as bitcoin's price continues to be below its peak.

summary: The director of the Center for AI Standards and Innovation has resigned, marking another departure from the role. This comes after David Sacks previously left his position as AI czar. The frequent turnover may impact the development of AI standards and innovation.

title: Adobe camera app’s new feature will critique your photos using AI
